provisions.title: Short title
provisions.content: These regulations may be cited as the Optometry (General) Regulations, 2011 and shall come into operation on the date of publication.

provisions.title: Interpretations
provisions.content: In these regulations, unless the context otherwise requires— “ Act ” means the Optometry Act; “ clinic ” has the meaning ascribed to it under the Act ; “ Conflict of interest ” means an action or a relationship which conflicts, or may reasonably be perceived to be conflict with a practitioner’s duty to ensure that all professional decisions are made in patient’s best interest and without reference to other criteria and includes a situation or circumstance described in regulation (16) ; “ facility ” has the meaning ascribed to it under the Act ; “ Practitioner ” means a registered Optometrist or Enrolled Ophthalmic Optician; “ professional misconduct ” means an action or relationships described in the regulation 15 . Part II – Practice

provisions.title: Application for registration
provisions.content: (1) A person who intends to practice as the optometry practitioner shall apply for registration to the council and pay the prescribed fees. (2) The council shall, upon receipt of an application under sub-regulation (1) , direct the Registrar to investigate on the applicant qualifications and advice the council before it take a decision on the application. (3) The council may, after receiving the advice of the Registrar, register the applicant or refuse to register the applicant and it shall, within thirty days from the date of refusal, communicate to the applicant the reasons for the refusal of his registration.

provisions.title: Application for enrollment
provisions.content: (1) A person who intends to be enrolled as the Ophthalmic Optician shall apply for enrollment to the council and pay the prescribed fees. (2) The council shall, upon receipt of an application under sub-regulation (1) , direct the Registrar to investigate on the applicant qualifications and advice the council before it take a decision on the application. (3) The council may, after receiving the advice of the Registrar, enroll the applicant or refuse to enroll the applicant and it shall, within thirty days from the date of refusal, communicate to the applicant the reasons for the refusal of his enrollment. (4) The annual retention fee shall be payable before first January of each year.

provisions.title: License practice
provisions.content: (1) A registered optmetry practioner or an enrolled Ophthalmic optician shall apply for practicing license to the council. (2) The applicant under sub-regulation (1) shall be accompanied by application fees and certificate of registration or of enrollment.

provisions.title: Renewal of license and reinstatement
provisions.content: (1) Every practioner authorized by the council to practicing optometry shall, within three months before the expire date of the apply for licence, apply for renewal of his license and pay the prescribed fee. (2) The licensee shall ensure that the council has the current address. (3) The practitioner shall, within thirty days after the change of his address or names, furnished the council with such information. (4) A practitioner whose license has lapsed for more than one year and wishes to resume practice shall apply for reinstatement to council. (5) The Council may, upon the receipt of an application under sub-regulation (4) grant reinstatement to practice provided that— (a) the applicant can demonstrate continue competence; (b) the applicant has satisfied current requirements for continuing education for the period which the license has been lapsed, not to exceed two years; and (c) the applicant has paid the prescribed reinstatement application fee. (6) Any person whose practicing licence lapsed and continuing practicing without renewing that licence commits an offence and shall be liable to a fines stipulated in the Act . (7) The Council may require an applicant whose license has expired and who cannot demonstrate continuing competency to sit for the council approved examinations before his licence can be reinstated.

provisions.title: Notice of cancellation of registration
provisions.content: (1) Where the Registrar has written a letter to a registered practitioner inquiring whether the registered practitioner has ceased to practice or has changed the residence or left Tanzania and has no intention of returning to practice or, where no answer has been returned to that letter within a period of six months, the Registrar may, by a registered letter addressed to the address appearing in the register, give notice to the practitioner that his name has been removed from the register. (2) The notice of an intention to remove the name of a practitioner from the register shall be deemed to have been served on the person concerned on the expiration of a period of thirty days from the date of the posting of the letter and the Registrar shall erase the name of the practitioner from the register.

provisions.title: Optometry facility
provisions.content: (1) A person who intends to establish optometry facility shall apply for licence to the council in the form setout in fourth schedule. (2) The council shall, upon the receipt of the application, under sub-regulation (1) , direct the Registrar to inspect the intended establishment and advise the council before it makes its decision. (3) An optometry facility shall comprise of— (a) optical laboratories; (b) optical surfacing unit; (c) optical factory and manufacturing; and (d) supplying and distributing unit of optical appliances. (4) The application shall specify the type and business applied.

provisions.title: Establishment of clinics
provisions.content: (1) A person who intends to establish optometry clinic shall apply for licence to the council in the form setout in fourth schedule. (2) The council shall, upon the receipt of the application, under sub regulation (1) , direct the Registrar to inspect the intended clinic and advise the council before it makes its decision. (3) The clinic shall abide to the standard requirements prescribed in the nineth schedule. (4) The appointed supervisor from the office of Regional Medical Officer or District Medical Officer shall inspect, approve and endorse the application.

provisions.title: Prohibition of rendering optometry clinic , facility or practice in contact lenses
provisions.content: (1) A person shall not render optometry facility or optometry clinic , unless that person has been approved, registered or enrolled and licensed by the Council. (2) A person shall not practice contact lenses unless approved by the Council. (3) Any person who contravenes this regulation commits an offence. Part III – Standards of practice, professional misconduct and conflict of interest

provisions.title: General standards of practice
provisions.content: (1) A practitioner shall exercise generally accepted standards of practice and procedures in the determination of the physical, optical, sensory, occulomotor state of the eye and adnexa, the assessment and care of a patient’s vision. (2) A practitioner shall, in exercising the functions under sub-regulation (1) — (a) keep clinical records with respect to his patients containing: (i) the patient’s history; (ii) the examination procedure used; (iii) the clinical findings obtained; (iv) the treatment prescribed and provided; (v) the practitioner’s fees and charges; and (vi) any other information that the Council may require; (b) have in his office the usual and necessary instruments, equipment and physical facilities for the provision of the diagnostic and treatment services performed by a practitioner; and (c) participate in a programme of continuing education, within a designated time, as required by the Council.

provisions.title: Roles and responsibilities of practitioners
provisions.content: (1) A registered optometrist shall perform roles and responsibilities setout in the sixth schedule. (2) An Enrolled ophthalmic optician shall perform roles and responsibilities of setout in the seventh schedule.

provisions.title: Minimum requirements for establishment of a facility
provisions.content: The minimum standard requirements for establishment of the facility are setout in the eighth schedule.

provisions.title: Standard equipment and instruments for a clinic
provisions.content: The Standard equipments and instruments for operating the clinics are setout in the nineth schedule.

provisions.title: Professional misconduct
provisions.content: The following acts, for the purpose of maintaining the integrity of optometry profession, constitute professional misconduct — (a) failing to abide by the terms, conditions or limitations of a license to practice optometry; (b) failing to maintain the standards of practice of the profession prescribed by the Council; (c) failing to maintain the records that are required to be kept under these regulations; (d) exceeding the lawful scope of the practice of optometry; (e) using, in conjunction with the practice of optometry, medicine other than those prescribed by the Council; (f) engaging in the practice of optometry while having a conflict of interest; (g) using terms, titles or designations other than those authorized by the Act or Regulations; (h) treating or attempting to treat eye problem which is beyond the practitioner’s experience or competence; (i) permitting or assisting any person who is not licensed under the Act to engage in the practice of optometry; (j) using in any way the name of another practitioner with respect to the practitioner’s own practice for more than three years; (k) communicating information that is not accurate, misleading, verifiable or relevant in the choice of a practitioner or that— (i) uses testimonial, comparative or superlative statements; (ii) endorses any product, appliance or instrument used in the practice of optometry; (iii) when communicating a practitioner’s fees and charges to the public patient or third party, fails to disclose the exact nature of the service or ophthalmic appliance provided for the fees and charges stated, (iv) is part of any communication, advertisement, promotion or offering by persons or corporate entities other than a practitioner of any product or service. (l) signing or issuing a certificate, report or similar document in connection with the practice of Optometry that contains a statement which the practitioner knows or ought to know is false, misleading or otherwise improper; (m) disclosing information concerning a patient to any person other than the patient without the consent of that patient or required to do so by law; (n) falsifying a record in respect of the observation or treatment of a patient; (o) failing without reasonable cause to continue to provide professional services to a patient; (p) conviction for an offence that in the opinion of the Council affects the fitness of a practitioner to engage in the practice of Optometry; (q) engaging in the practice of Optometry while the practitioner’s ability is impaired by alcohol or drugs; (r) failing without reasonable cause to make available to a patient on request a written, signed and dated ophthalmic appliance prescription containing all necessary relevant clinical and ophthalmic specifications; (s) conducting and act relevant to the practice of Optometry that, having regard to all circumstances, would reasonably be regarded as disgraceful, dishonest, unprofessional or otherwise unbecoming of a practitioner; (t) failing to perform all necessary tests to determine the sustainability of a client to wear contact lenses; (u) failing to provide necessary aftercare to contact lens patient; and (v) failing to conduct practice of optometry in an authorized premises and standards as prescribed by the Council.

provisions.title: Conflict of interest
provisions.content: A conflict of interest may occur when a practitioner is or comes to be in a situation which— (a) by connection with the practitioner’s practice, resulting in a gain, financial or otherwise, other than that earned from the performance of professional services in the practitioner’s practice; (b) places the practitioner’s professional integrity or rendering of services at risk of being controlled or influenced by other persons, business entities, corporations or factors other than the practitioner’s professional judgment of what is best for the patient, in consideration of generally accepted standards of practice; or (c) impedes the patient’s ability to make an informed decision regarding consent to treatment or the purchase of services or materials related to the practitioner’s recommendations for care. Part IV – Complaints

provisions.title: Complaints
provisions.content: (1) Any person may make a complaint in respect of the conduct of a practitioner or institution to the Registrar and the complaint shall be dealt within accordance with the provisions of the Act and these Regulations. (2) A complaint against a practitioner shall be in writing, signed by the complainant and be referred to the Registrar. (3) The Registrar shall, upon the receipt of a complaint, conduct a preliminary investigation to the complaint and may appoint at least two people to assist in the investigation. (4) The Registrar shall send a notice of conducting preliminary investigation in writing to the investigated practitioner or institution.

provisions.title: Powers of Registrar on preliminary investigation
provisions.content: (1) The Registrar may conducting a preliminary investigation— (a) require the investigated practitioner or institution to produce prescriptions, samples, inventory and other reports, books, papers or other documents or records in the investigated person’s possession or under his control; (b) make copies of the documents or records that are produced; (c) question and obtain further particulars from the complainant, the investigated practitioner, institutions or other person relating to the complaint; and (d) enter and inspect a practitioner’s office, or other premises at a reasonable time. (2) The Registrar shall report the complaints against the practitioner or institution to Ethical and Disciplinary Committee on all matters regarding the professional conduct of that practitioner or institution. (3) A practitioner who or an institution which fails to comply with a request made under this regulation commits an offence.

provisions.title: Composition and powers of Ethical and Disciplinary Committee
provisions.content: (1) There is established a Committee to be known as Ethical and Disciplinary Committee whose members shall be appointed by the Council. (2) The Committee shall be composed of not less than three members as follows— (a) a person representing the Tanzania Optometric Association; (b) a person representing any other Council under the Ministry of Health and Social Welfare; (c) a legal qualified person, who is not a Member of the Council; and (d) any other person who the Council may determine. (3) The Council shall appoint from the members of the Ethical and Disciplinary Committee a Chairperson of that Committee. (4) The Committee members shall appoint one member among themselves to act as a Chairperson in the absence of a Chairperson. (5) The Council may in the event of death, resignation or removal of a member of the Ethical and Disciplinary Committee appoint another person to replace that member who shall serve the remainder of the term of office of the committee. (6) The term of office of a member of the Ethical and Disciplinary Committee shall be a period of three years from the date of appointment and members may be reappointed for additional terms.

provisions.title: Co-opted members
provisions.content: The Ethical and Disciplinary Committee may, when dealing with any technical matter, seek the services of counsel, consultants or experts whom it considers necessary to assist and advice on the matter the committee is dealt with.

provisions.title: Complaint dismissal
provisions.content: (1) The Ethical and Disciplinary Committee may, where its satisfied that the complaint against the practitioner of an institute is baseless and before the completion of a preliminary investigation, dismiss a complaint as being frivolous or vexatious. (2) Where a complaint has been dismissed under subregulation (1) Registrar shall notify the complainant in writing of the dismissal. (3) Where a complaint has been dismissed under subregulation (1) the complainant may, within fourteen days after receiving the notice of dismissal, in writing appeal to the Council.

provisions.title: Report of findings
provisions.content: The Registrar shall, upon concluding the preliminary investigation or required investigation, report his findings to the Ethical and Disciplinary Committee.

provisions.title: Report required
provisions.content: The Ethical and Disciplinary Committee may recommend to the Council for disciplinary inquiry upon consideration of the report submitted before it under regulation 22 . Part V – Disciplinary procedure

provisions.title: Inquiries
provisions.content: The Council may, after receipt of the report of the Ethical and Disciplinary Committee’s investigation into a complaint, order the committee to conduct further inquiries and report to the Council within a specified time.

provisions.title: Council to Conduct Inquiry
provisions.content: (1) Where the Council conducts an inquiry under Section 25 of the Act , it shall be served on the investigated practitioner or an Institution and on the complainant which has led to the hearing. (2) The Registrar shall, before the inquiry, give a Practitioner whose conduct is being investigated the opportunity to examine written reports or other records in the possession of the Registrar that are intended to be produced or to form part of the evidence of that inquiry. (3) The notice required by Sub-Regulation (1) , shall be served either personally, or sent to by registered mail directed to the last address of the Complainant and Investigated Practitioner known to the Registrar, not less than 14 days before the date established for an inquiry. (4) The notice shall include a statement that the Council may proceed to hear the matter in the absence of either or both of the complainant, investigated Practitioner or Institution should either fail to appear at the time and place set for the inquiry.

provisions.title: Failure to appear
provisions.content: Where a complainant, an investigated practitioner or institution served with a notice of inquiry fails to appear for an inquiry and the council has not received an acceptable explanation for the absence, the council may proceed with the inquiry.

provisions.title: Evidence
provisions.content: (1) The rules of evidence applicable in civil proceedings in the Court of law are applicable at an inquiry and affidavit or statutory declaration of a person is admissible in evidence subject to the requirements, the Council may impose with respect to the presence of the deponent or declaring for the purpose of examination and cross examination on the admitted document. (2) Oral evidence given at an inquiry shall be recorded and a written transcript of the evidence be prepared. [Cap 33]

provisions.title: Hearing in public
provisions.content: (1) An inquiry may, at the description of the council and upon the request by the investigated practitioner, institute or the complainant, be held in the public. (2) A request for hearing in public shall be submitted to the Council at least thirty days before the fixed day for in public the inquiry.

provisions.title: Findings of die Council
provisions.content: The findings of the Council shall base on the evidence adduced at the inquiry.

provisions.title: Return of records
provisions.content: The records tendered as evidence at an inquiry shall, upon the request of the person who tendered them be returned to that person by the Council after the matter in issue has been finally determined.

provisions.title: Council decision
provisions.content: (1) The Council shall, within thirty days after final consideration of any matter brought before it, give his decision in writing and serve the complainant, investigated practitioner or institution with a copy of that decision. (2) The council shall communicate his decision to practitioner in person, by registered mail or by electronic mail. (3) The Registrar may give notice of a decision of the Council to other persons that the Council considers appropriate.

provisions.title: Cancellation and Suspension
provisions.content: The Registrar shall, upon receipt of a cancelled or suspended licence, note on the license the fact for the cancellation or suspension and retain at licence.

provisions.title: Record of proceeding
provisions.content: The Council shall compile a record of proceeding in which an inquiry has been held which includes- (a) the complaint by which the proceeding was commenced; (b) the notice of inquiry; (c) all documentary evidence filed with the Council; (d) the transcript of the oral evidence given at the inquiry; (e) the findings of the Council and the reasons for findings: and (f) the decision of the Council.

provisions.title: Appeal
provisions.content: (1) Any decision of the Council regarding the application for registration, enrollment, deregistration, or removal from the register or the roll shall be stated categorically and the reasons for such decision be given as may enable a person in respect of whom a decision has been made to understand the nature of decision. (2) A decision of the Council shall be communicated to a concerned person by a registered mail. (3) A person aggrieved by the decision of the Council may appeal to the Minister within thirty days from the date of the decision. Part VI – General provision

provisions.title: Council meetings
provisions.content: (1) The Chairperson of the Council shall determine time and place where meetings of the Council shall be held. (2) Meetings of the council shall be held at least once in every six months. (3) The registrar shall, at the request Chairperson of the Council, issue a written notice of not less than two weeks to all Council members of the time and place of each meeting of the Council. (4) The Chairperson of the Council and the Registrar shall prepare a proposed agenda for every meeting. (5) The proposed agenda referred to in sub regulation (4) may be reviewed and revised by the of members Council at the commencement of each meeting of the Council. (6) Meetings of the Council shall be conducted, as nearly as possible, in conformity with the Act . (7) Every matter submitted to a meeting of the Council for decision making shall be decided by votes a majority of the and in the case of an equality of votes, the Chairperson shall have the deciding vote.

provisions.title: Register and roll
provisions.content: (1) The register and roll shall be in the form prescribed by the Council. (2) A practitioner shall notify the Registrar in writing of any changes in the information contained in the register or roll relating to that practitioner within three months from the time the change occurs. (3) The Registrar shall annually publish in the Gazette the names and particulars of practitioners as required by the Act .

provisions.title: Committees
provisions.content: (1) The Council may form any committee it considers necessary to assist in its operations. (2) The Registrar shall by virtue of his position be a Secretary of all committees formed by the Council.

provisions.title: Offence and penalties
provisions.content: Any person who contravenes the provisions of these Regulations commits any office and upon conviction is liable to a penalty specified in the Act .

provisions.title: Inspection and supervision
provisions.content: The inspection and supervision shall be conducted by the Regional or District Optometrists or Ophthalmic Optician with the collaboration of Regional Health Management Teams or Council Health Management Teams.

provisions.title: Claim for reimbursement
provisions.content: (1) Members of the Council shall be reimbursed for reasonable expenses incurred by them while attending Council meetings and business at a rate set by the Council. (2) Reasonable expenses incurred by Council members shall be reimbursed under sub-regulation (1) when submitted in a written form to the Council. (3) The individuals appointed under sub-regulation (1) shall be reimbursed for expenses incurred on Council business to the extent that Council members are entitled to be reimbursed under this regulation. First Schedule Registration application form [Please note: Forms have not been reproduced. Please refer to the publication document.] Second Schedule Enrollment application form [Please note: Forms have not been reproduced. Please refer to the publication document.] Third Schedule Application form for licence to practice [Please note: Forms have not been reproduced. Please refer to the publication document.] Fourth Schedule Application form to establishment of optometry facility [Please note: Forms have not been reproduced. Please refer to the publication document.] Fifth Schedule Application form to establishment of optometry clinic [Please note: Forms have not been reproduced. Please refer to the publication document.] Sixth Schedule Roles and responsibilities of optometrists It shall the duty of every Optometrist to: • To maintain/create good interpersonal relationship with a patient • Assess the visual status of the patients • Perform a comprehensive eye examination • Diagnosis and correct the visual abnormalities of the patients • Dispense and provide Visual aids to patients. • Verify Rxs prescribed from other practitioners • Rehabilitate patients with low Vision • Screen, conduct outreach program and provide eye health education to the community and occupational. • Refer all cases which needs special care • Perform first aid and arrange for next action. • Train, supervise optometry students and other junior staff • Carry out routine maintenance/repair of equipment and instruments • Repair spectacles • Fit and assess C/L • Asses Binocular Vision and correct its abnormalities. • Perform other specialty care as prescribed by ophthalmologist. Seventh Schedule Roles and responsibilities of the ophthalmic optician It shall the duty of every ophthalmic Optician to: • Create good interpersonal relationship with a patient • Assess the visual status of the patients • Do eye examination • Correct the visual problems of the patients and dispense optical corrections. • Verify Rxs prescribed from other practitioners. • Screen, conduct outreach programs. • Refer all case which needs special care. • Perform first aid and arrange for the next action. • Train other junior staff. • Carry out routine preventive maintenance of equipments/instruments. • Repair spectacles. • Perform other specialty care as prescribed by optometrists. Eighth Schedule Minimum requirements for establishment of optomentry facility A – Optical manufacturing factory and optical surfacing unit A minimum requirement of Industrial regulations of the United Republic of Tanzania applies. B – Optical laboratory • Grinding Machine. • Ophthalmic heater. • Optical pliers. • Assorted pliers • A set of screw drivers. • Drilling machine. • Lensometer. • Grooving Machine. • Polishing machine. • Tinting Cabinet. • PD Ruler. • Running Water. • First Aid Kit. • Ventilated Room. C – Supplying and distributing unit of optical appliance • Certificate of approval from the council. • Wide range of optical appliance. • Stock evidence. • Optical appliances shall not be mixed with other commodities. • Should supply to registered practitioners only. Nineth Schedule Standard equipment for Operating an optometric clinic Premises • Conducive area, quite and not hazardous area. • At least two rooms • Testing distance in the room with 6M or 3M with reversal mirror. • Should be located at safe area and easily reachable by all clients considering age and physical status. Clinical Equipment/Instruments 1. Test charts - distance and near charts 2. Trail case with all accessories, including cross cylinder 3. Trial frame 4. Retinoscope 5. Ophthalmoscope 6. Tonometer 7. Lensometer 8. Torch 9. color vision tester 10. Steriopsis 11. PD Ruler 12. Sink with running water Tenth Schedule Check list for supervision • Number of qualifying staff. (Registered optometrist/Enrolled ophthalmic optician is mandatory). • Practitioner registration and License to practice. • Location. • Well arranged clinic. • Enough working equipments (Refer 9 th schedule). • Testing distance. • Prescription forms. • Patients record keeping.
